Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 21:01 - Our engineers have now restored power to all but three customers in the Tunbridge Wells, TN3 and TN7 area, by diverting power around the overhead cable fault. We know a power cut can be difficult; we're sorry for any disruption this unplanned power cut caused.  

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations.

To talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 20:47 - Our engineers have confirmed that they plan to restore some supplies by connecting a temporary generator. The remaining properties will be restored by diverting power around the network.

To allow time for this, we're now estimating we'll have power back on between 01:00 and 02:00. Some properties may be back on sooner than others but we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 20:29 - Thanks for your patience this evening.

Our staff are currently checking our network equipment throughout the area. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 01:44 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 1534 customers in the Esher area by diverting power around an underground cable fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.
.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Bev at 01:00 - I appreciate your continued patience during the power cut today. Our staff are working as quickly as they can to get the lights back on, and they've advised us that there are now 84 properties still affected. They have managed to get a further 330 of our customers back on by diverting electricity, so your power may have come on sooner than we originally advised. If you're still off, please don't worry; work on the issue will continue along with SMS updates until everyone is back on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Bev at 00:49 - We have safely diverted some power, restoring a further 331 properties. This leaves 414 properties affected by the underground cable fault. Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our network. In addition to this, they are continuing to liaise with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored. You may be worried about the food in your fridge and freezer. Food should keep for up to 6 hours in the fridge, and between 15-24 hours in the freezer if you can avoid opening them. If your power is back on and you wish to stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.
